# Take-Home Document And Commit

## When to Use
- When polishing submission.
- After implementation is complete.

## Procedure
1. Ensure `README.md` explains what the project does.
2. Add setup and run instructions.
3. Describe repository structure by file/module responsibility.
4. Note assumptions, trade-offs, and known limitations.
5. Add "if I had more time" improvements where appropriate.
6. Ensure comments/docstrings exist for non-obvious logic.
7. Prepare concise, meaningful commit messages grouped by logical changes.

## Deliverable Checklist
- README is complete and accurate.
- Decisions and trade-offs are documented.
- Commit history is readable and intentional.
